intro: Graphistylis serrana, also known as Brazilian tansy or Brazilian buttonbush, is a flowering plant species native to Brazil. The leaves of Graphistylis serrana have been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Graphistylis serrana include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
